creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace that lets non-technical users ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion. The tool generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent executions deterministic and cost-efficient.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google that replaces DOM-based agent navigation with structured tools. Agents can call named, typed, described actions directly rather than guessing site behavior through screenshots and pixel math. The standard supports both declarative HTML attributes and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom tools.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser presents a framework for delegating software development tasks to AI agents while minimizing human attention overhead. The stack includes signal agents that monitor communication channels, verification gates for safety, weekly meta-analysis of conversation history for skill discovery, and biometric integration to prevent developer burnout.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford argues that tool discipline and behavior training matter more than model scale for query tasks. A 4B Snorkel-finetuned model with RL outperforms Qwen 3 235B by using proper tool invocation (get_table_name, schema inspection, self-correction) rather than reasoning depth, achieving this with under $500 training cost.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
videos.summary: Cole Medin conducts a live stream testing Anthropic's Fable 5, examining whether the model's capabilities justify its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8. The stream puts the model through real-world tasks to assess its practical value proposition.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables building and deploying AI agents to production in minutes. The tool provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade deployment.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin hosts a live AI Q&A stream on Saturdays where viewers can ask about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream combines technical discussion with casual chess gameplay as entertainment.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a multi-model development workflow that routes planning and reasoning to Claude Opus while delegating UI design to Gemini 3.5 Flash. The approach leverages each model's relative strengths: Opus for complex reasoning and integration logic, Gemini for visual frontend generation, with handoff documents enabling task composition across models.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to construct proactive AI agents that can autonomously improve system performance through feedback loops. Key use cases include autonomous ads optimization and SEO, enabled by memory and tool integration through frameworks like Loopany.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and autonomous coding capabilities, comparing progress to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers how goal-setting features work in coding assistants, setup methods, and long-horizon task execution through /Mission exploration.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is described as a new AI coding paradigm. The video covers what Symphony is, how it works, harness setup with auto testing capabilities, and WORKFLOW.md configuration for Symphony setup.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason demonstrates agent-related tools and frameworks including autoresearch capabilities, Claude Code's memory architecture, and self-evolving mechanisms in Hermes and OpenClaw. The content covers practical agent implementations and skill development patterns.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models that combine hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE architectures. The video explains how these components differentiate Nemotron 3 from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that implements sparse attention (MSA or MiniMax Sparse Attention) instead of full attention. It includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is an agent framework that features a unique harness layer architecture. The video compares Pi against other coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how increasing product complexity affects harness layer design.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as part of the ongoing hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video covers the roles of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in advancing chip manufacturing capabilities.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discuss strategies for standing out in the 2026 tech job market as AI screens resumes. The video covers networking, personal branding, the Feynman Principle, and argues that junior developers will evolve rather than be replaced, drawing parallels to how transportation jobs transformed rather than disappeared.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production best practices for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with OAuth and IAM, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. The recommendations emphasize scaling from localhost prototypes to production systems handling real users.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: Tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. Covers setting up DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway via CDK compared to manual console configuration, with deployment, testing, and teardown examples.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS presents five techniques to prevent AI hallucinations and improve agent reliability: semantic tool selection to reduce context window bloat, graph RAG for precise structured queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails via Python code to enforce rules, and runtime steering for agent self-correction. Demonstrations use a travel agent built with Strands Agents framework.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video essay examining whether Google engineers use "vibing" (casual, intuitive coding) versus structured approaches. It compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities and explores coding culture at Google across 2024-2026, including references to Google Tech Island.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company described as potentially ending open source development. The video explores how Malus operates and raises questions about its impact on open source maintainers and the sustainability of the open source model.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: A creator documents an experiment where Claude Code completed their coding work more effectively than they could. The video explores whether AI will replace human developers across all tasks, framed as both serious concern and clickable premise.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early adopters are not proof of product-market fit and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates for embedding in customer workflows over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird founded Koah Labs, an ad monetization platform for AI tools, raising $26M. He argues that subscriptions have failed for AI applications and that ads will drive adoption, while the real bottleneck for AI agents is human trust rather than technology.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapped path to $10M ARR without external funding. He covers strategies for reaching $1M ARR in 117 days, product-led growth, SEO, churn reduction, pricing experiments, and decision-making frameworks for founders.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh argues that teaching AI fundamentals by hand at human speed, grounded in mathematical foundations rather than the latest tools, builds durable skills that survive hype cycles. He contends that people shape AI rather than the reverse, and warns against the "AI-Native" trap of becoming dependent on current tools.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5 as a second brain for AI entrepreneurs, comparing it to Mythos 5 and covering loop engineering for recursive self-improvement, memory systems for long-running tasks, and emerging opportunities in agentic payments. The episode explores how smaller models balance capabilities with guardrails for regulated industries.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
